description: Applies the §8.1 version acceptance and refusal rules: refuses a version outside the tool's declared supported range in either direction — a higher major than the tool supports (and, while the spec is pre-1.0, a higher minor), and likewise a version below the tool's minimum (pre-1.0: any unsupported lower minor); compares by SemVer 2.0.0 precedence so build metadata is ignored and a higher patch within a supported minor is accepted; and does not accept a prerelease unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease.
notes: Tests assume the SDK under test supports a version range bounded below `999.x` for major-version refusal cases, and bounded below `0.999.x` for pre-1.0 minor-version refusal cases. SDK conformance to OBI-T-04 is independent of which specific version the SDK supports; the test asserts the refusal behavior on documents declaring versions higher than any plausible SDK release in this version family. Downward refusal (a document declaring a version below the SDK's minimum MUST be refused, per §8.1's 'refusal runs downward as well') is fixtured with `requiresMinSupported`: those tests apply only to SDKs whose MinSupportedVersion is at or above the annotation's value, and are skipped (reported separately) for SDKs whose range extends down to the document's version. Acceptance-presuming positives are gated with `requiresSupports` (the acceptance-gate annotation; see the corpus README): each is administered only to tools whose OBI-T-04 acceptance predicate accepts the annotation's version, because which versions a tool accepts is its own support declaration (§8.1), never a corpus assumption — a conformant tool supporting exactly {0.2.0} must refuse a 0.2.1 document, and gating on the tested-range declarations instead would make the reference SDKs (MaxTested 0.2.0, accepting 0.2.1 via their own line declaration) skip tests they pass. The current-version and build-metadata cases carry `requiresSupports: "0.2.0"`; the higher-patch case carries `requiresSupports: "0.2.1"`, so for a tool declaring a release line it checks the document-processing path honors that declaration end-to-end. The prerelease case stays ungated: a prerelease, which sorts below its release, MUST NOT be accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (`0.2.0-rc.1` asserts default rejection by a tool that has not opted in; the opt-in exception is not separately fixtured).

tests.description: document declares the current spec version (administered only to tools whose acceptance predicate accepts 0.2.0)
build metadata is ignored for comparison; `0.2.0+build.1` is treated as `0.2.0` and accepted
a higher patch within a supported minor is accepted as non-breaking
a prerelease is not accepted unless the tool declares support for that specific prerelease (default rejection)
document declares a major version higher than any plausible SDK supports
document declares a far-future major version with non-zero minor
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor than any plausible SDK supports (per §8.1 finer-granularity rule)
pre-1.0 document declares a higher minor with a patch component
post-1.0 forward-compat: document declares a same-major-higher-minor version (SDK supporting major 1 should accept per §8.1)
downward refusal: pre-1.0 document declares a lower minor below the SDK's minimum supported version (refused rather than processed under the wrong rules, per §8.1)
downward refusal: a higher patch on an unsupported lower minor is still below the minimum (patch never rescues a refused minor)
